ddls:
CREATE TABLE "life_tables" (
  "life_table_id" TEXT /* Unique life table identifier */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the life table */,
  "population_type" TEXT /* Type of population (e.g., general population, insured population) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region */,
  "source" TEXT /* Data source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date of last update */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range in years */,
  "qx" REAL /* Probability of dying within the age range */,
  "Ix" REAL /* Number of survivors at the beginning of the age interval */,
  "dx" REAL /* Number of deaths within the age range */,
  "Lx" REAL /* Person-years lived within the age range */,
  "Tx" REAL /* Total number of person-years remaining */,
  "ex" REAL /* Life expectancy at the beginning of the age range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("life_table_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique user identifier */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for authentication (hashed) */,
  "role" TEXT /* User role (e.g., administrator, actuary, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policyholders" (
  "policyholder_id" TEXT /* Unique policyholder identifier */,
  "policy_number" TEXT /* Insurance policy number */,
  "age" TEXT /* Age of the policyholder */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the policyholder (male, female, other) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policyholder_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" TEXT /* Unique visualization identifier */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph, table) */,
  "life_table_id" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the life table used in the visualization */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the user who created the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policyholder_life_table_link" (
  "policyholder_id" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the policyholder */,
  "life_table_id" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the life table */,
  "link_date" TEXT /* Date the policyholder was linked to the life table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policyholder_id", "life_table_id")
)
column_names: Type of population (e.g., general population, insured population)
Probability of dying within the age range
Number of survivors at the beginning of the age interval
Number of deaths within the age range
Person-years lived within the age range
Life expectancy at the beginning of the age range
Gender of the policyholder (male, female, other)
Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph, table)
Foreign key referencing the life table used in the visualization
Foreign key referencing the user who created the visualization
Foreign key referencing the life table
Date the policyholder was linked to the life table
